notes: Source is the official QSC/Q-SYS help documentation (v9.8). The Core 110f is a 1RU device (44mm / 1U). It has two major hardware revisions: the original (v1) and Core 110f v2. The v2 removes: 16x16 GPIO connectors, front panel OLED display, and front panel USB Type-A ports; it replaces them with a simplified solution. This structured output describes the original Core 110f (v1). The POTS connector is physically an RJ11 (6P2C) — closest schema match is rj45 but they are different form factors; noted accordingly. The HDMI port is documented as 'for future use' with no version or direction specified — grade omitted. The 6 USB-A host ports and USB-B device port are present on original v1; v2 removes front USB-A ports. All 24 analog audio channels use 3.5mm 3-position euroblock connectors (not XLR). Ethernet is confirmed dual Gigabit (1G). RS232 uses a 3-pin 5mm euroblock. GPIO uses 10-position 3.5mm euroblock connectors.

ports.name: Mic/Line In 1
ports.note: 3.5mm 3-position euroblock, balanced/unbalanced, phantom power available; channels 1–8 share same connector style

ports.name: Flex 1
ports.note: 3.5mm 3-position euroblock; each of 8 Flex channels independently software-configurable as mic/line input or line output with phantom power on input

ports.name: LAN A
ports.note: Primary Q-LAN / Q-SYS Control / VoIP / AES67 network connection

ports.name: LAN B
ports.note: Secondary Gigabit Ethernet; supports redundancy or additional network functions

ports.name: USB Type-A 1 (Front)
ports.note: Front panel; spec notes 'for future use'

ports.name: USB Type-A 2 (Front)
ports.note: Front panel; spec notes 'for future use'

ports.name: USB Type-A 3 (Rear)
ports.note: Rear panel; spec notes 'for future use'

ports.name: USB Type-A 4 (Rear)
ports.note: Rear panel; spec notes 'for future use'

ports.name: USB Type-A 5 (Rear)
ports.note: Rear panel; spec notes 'for future use'

ports.name: USB Type-A 6 (Rear)
ports.note: Rear panel; spec notes 'for future use'

ports.name: USB Type-B (Device)
ports.note: Rear panel USB-B device port; supports up to 16x16 USB audio + Q-SYS camera/AV bridging

ports.name: GPI (GPIO Inputs)
ports.note: 10-position 3.5mm euroblock; 16 general-purpose inputs, 0–24V analog or contact closure. Not present on Core 110f v2.

ports.name: GPO (GPIO Outputs)
ports.note: 10-position 3.5mm euroblock; 16 general-purpose open-collector outputs (24V, 0.2A max). Not present on Core 110f v2.

ports.name: POTS
ports.note: RJ11 (6P2C) analog telephone line connection; connector physically is RJ11 but closest in schema is rj45 — actual connector is RJ11/6P2C

ports.name: HDMI
ports.note: Marked 'for future use' in QSC documentation; version and direction not specified

ports.name: AC In
ports.note: IEC C13/C14 inlet; 100–240V~ 50–60Hz, 150W universal power supply
